decision.recommended_next_action: Use the saved LLM evaluation artifacts to guide stronger-model or calibration work only if a separate, concrete benchmark is explicitly desired.
decision.stop_reason: This branch completed its validation target by running a local LLM over the saved 150-example slice, adding 30 gold spot checks, and comparing lexical versus import-neighborhood contexts on accuracy and calibration.

recommended_next_action: Use the saved LLM evaluation artifacts to guide stronger-model or calibration work only if a separate, concrete benchmark is explicitly desired.
run_notes_tail: - Local model: `Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct` resolved from cached Hugging Face snapshot `[redacted-local-hf-cache]/hub/models--Qwen--Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct/snapshots/aa8e72537993ba99e69dfaafa59ed015b17504d1`.
- Resource snapshot: `nvidia-smi` GPU utilization sampled at 95%; `/proc/meminfo` reported MemAvailable during run completion; process max RSS captured in the JSON report.
- Checked 30 stratified weak labels against `[redacted-local-vllm-checkout]` source.
- 30/30 labels had the requested `def`/`class` near the labelled target line, so no weak-label kill condition was triggered.
| Packer | Strict file-line acc | ±2-line acc | Signature+path acc | Exact signature acc | Answerable abstain | No-answer abstain | False answer on no-answer | Brier |

By type, import-neighborhood beat lexical on strict exact-signature accuracy for import-definition (14.0% vs 0.0%) and subclass-base (22.2% vs 2.8%), while imported call-sites remained difficult (1.6% exact for both). The LLM frequently selected nearby/decorated lines or partial signatures, so line-tolerant and signature+path metrics are more informative than strict line equality for this small model.

The branch objective is met: a real local LLM was run over the saved 150-example stratified slice with lexical versus import-neighborhood contexts, and gold spot checks were added. The context-packer hypothesis is supported directionally at LLM-answer level: import-neighborhood gives a +12.0 point strict file-line gain, +29.3 point ±2-line gain, +25.3 point signature+path gain, and +9.3 point exact-signature gain over lexical RAG. Absolute accuracy is much lower than the deterministic extractor, showing that a small 3B instruction model struggles to copy exact code navigation answers even when context contains the target. Calibration is mixed/negative for import-neighborhood because it false-answers 36.7% of no-answer probes and has worse Brier score than conservative lexical RAG.
- No project-local helper inference server was launched or left running.

Finalize positive for this branch because it acquired parent artifacts, ran the intended local LLM evaluation on the saved 150-example slice, added gold spot checks, and produced durable accuracy/calibration artifacts. Do not create a generic successor-of-successor branch from this routing branch.
